title: psoralea laxa
intro: Psoralea laxa, also known as Indian turnip or white goa bean, is a type of plant that belongs to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Psoralea laxa include its ability to improve immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and potentially lower cholesterol levels. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and its seeds are often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
